title: Introducing theSVG: The Open SVG Brand Library
excerpt: We launched theSVG with 4,000+ brand SVG icons. Free, open-source, and community-driven. Here is why we built it and what is next.
author: theSVG Team
body:
Today we are launching theSVG, an open-source library of 4,000+ brand SVG icons for developers and designers.

Every brand deserves a place. No gatekeeping, no approval queues, no politics. If a brand exists, its icon should be freely available.

## What you get

- **4,000+ brand icons** from sources like Simple Icons, svgl, and lobe-icons, merged and deduplicated
- **Multiple variants** per icon: color, mono, light, dark, wordmark
- **Every format**: SVG, JSX, Vue, HTML, CDN link, Data URI
- **npm packages**: @thesvg/react, @thesvg/vue, @thesvg/svelte, @thesvg/cli
- **MCP server** for AI assistants like Claude and Cursor
- **REST API** and jsDelivr CDN with zero auth required

## Why we built this

Existing icon libraries are fragmented. Simple Icons has great coverage but only mono variants. svgl has beautiful color icons but a smaller set. We merged the best of all worlds into one unified library.

## What is next

Cloud provider icons (AWS, Azure, GCP), a Figma plugin, and community submissions at scale. Stay tuned.

title: 739 Official AWS Architecture Icons Now Available
excerpt: Every AWS service, resource, and category icon from the official 2026-Q1 icon set. Lambda, EC2, S3, and 736 more.
author: theSVG Team
body:
We just added **739 official AWS Architecture Icons** to theSVG, sourced directly from the AWS 2026-Q1 icon package.

## What is included

- **300 service icons**: Lambda, EC2, S3, RDS, DynamoDB, Bedrock, and every AWS service
- **400 resource icons**: EC2 instances, S3 buckets, Lambda functions, and specific resource types
- **26 category icons**: Compute, Storage, Database, AI, and every AWS category with brand colors
- **13 group icons**: VPC, Region, Availability Zone, and other architecture diagram elements

## Multiple sizes

Each service icon comes in 16px, 32px, 48px (default), and 64px variants. Perfect for architecture diagrams at any scale.

## How to use

Browse the full collection at [thesvg.org/collection/aws](/collection/aws). Copy SVG, grab a CDN link, or install via npm:

```bash
npx @thesvg/cli add aws-aws-lambda
```

All icons are provided under CC-BY-ND 2.0 license from AWS.

title: Azure and Google Cloud Icons Join theSVG
excerpt: 626 Microsoft Azure service icons and 214 Google Cloud Platform icons are now available. theSVG is now the largest free cloud icon library.
author: theSVG Team
body:
Today we are adding two major cloud providers to theSVG: **Microsoft Azure** (626 icons) and **Google Cloud Platform** (214 icons).

Combined with our existing AWS collection, theSVG now hosts **1,579 cloud provider icons** across all three major platforms, making it the largest free cloud icon library available.

## Azure: 626 Service Icons

29 categories covering every Azure service: Compute, Networking, Databases, AI, Security, DevOps, IoT, and more. From Virtual Machines to Cosmos DB to Azure Functions.

Browse at [thesvg.org/collection/azure](/collection/azure).

## Google Cloud: 214 Service Icons

Auto-categorized into Compute, Storage, Analytics, Database, Networking, AI, Security, and more. Includes Compute Engine, BigQuery, Cloud Run, GKE, and all major GCP services.

Browse at [thesvg.org/collection/gcp](/collection/gcp).

## Cross-cloud search

The best part: search works across all providers. Type "compute" and see AWS EC2, Azure VMs, and GCP Compute Engine side by side. Type "database" and compare options across all three clouds.

## Total count: 5,500+

| Collection | Icons |
|-----------|-------|
| Brand Icons | 4,019 |
| AWS Architecture | 739 |
| Azure Services | 626 |
| Google Cloud | 214 |
| **Total** | **5,598** |

All icons are free to use. Azure icons under MIT license, GCP icons under Apache 2.0.

title: 5,500+ Icons: Building the Definitive SVG Icon Hub
excerpt: From brand logos to cloud architecture diagrams, theSVG now covers every icon a developer needs. Here is our vision for what comes next.
author: theSVG Team
body:
With 5,500+ icons across brands, AWS, Azure, and GCP, theSVG has grown from a brand icon library into something bigger: **the one place developers go for any SVG icon**.

## What makes theSVG different

- **One library, every icon type**: Brand logos, cloud service icons, and more, all in one search
- **Every format**: SVG, JSX, Vue, Svelte, CDN, CLI, MCP server
- **No gatekeeping**: Submit any icon, no approval politics
- **Developer-first**: npm packages, tree-shakeable, typed, zero config

## What we are working on next

- **Kubernetes and CNCF icons**: The cloud-native ecosystem deserves coverage
- **Figma plugin**: Insert icons directly into your designs
- **VS Code extension**: Search and insert icons while coding
- **Community submissions at scale**: Streamlined review and merge process
- **Icon comparison**: See AWS Lambda vs Azure Functions vs Cloud Functions side by side

## Get involved

theSVG is open source. Submit icons, report issues, or contribute code on [GitHub](https://github.com/GLINCKER/thesvg).

title: thesvg is Now on Raycast
excerpt: Search and copy any of our 5,600 icons directly from Raycast. No browser needed.
author: theSVG Team
body:
The thesvg Raycast extension is live on the Raycast Store.

## What it does

Search across all 5,600 icons from Raycast. Brands, AWS, Azure, GCP. Type a name, hit enter, get the SVG on your clipboard. No browser, no tab switching.

You can also:
- Copy the CDN URL instead of the raw SVG
- Filter by collection (brands, AWS, Azure, GCP)
- Preview icons before copying
- Open the icon detail page on thesvg.org

## Install

Open Raycast, search for "thesvg" in the Store, and install. Or visit the store page directly:

https://www.raycast.com/thegdsks/thesvg

## Why Raycast

When you need an icon while coding, opening a browser tab breaks your flow. With the Raycast extension you stay in your editor. Hit the hotkey, type "lambda", copy, paste. Done.

This is one of several integrations we're building. npm packages, CLI, MCP server for AI tools, and now Raycast. The goal is to get icons into your workflow wherever you already are.
